text: Astronomy
the study of space, stars, planets, galaxies, and the universe
original_text: Astronomy is the study of space, stars, planets, galaxies, and the universe.

text: asks what exists beyond earth and how those objects behave
original_text: Astronomy asks what exists beyond Earth and how those objects behave.

text: the universe contains stars, planets, moons, asteroids, comets, gas, dust, and galaxies
original_text: The universe contains stars, planets, moons, asteroids, comets, gas, dust, and galaxies.

text: A star is a massive ball of hot gas that produces energy through nuclear fusion
original_text: A star is a massive ball of hot gas that produces energy through nuclear fusion.

text: the Sun is the closest star to Earth
original_text: The Sun is the closest star to Earth.

text: planets orbit stars and are massive enough to be rounded by gravity
original_text: Planets orbit stars and are massive enough to be rounded by gravity.

text: moons are natural objects that orbit planets or dwarf planets
original_text: Moons are natural objects that orbit planets or dwarf planets.

text: asteroids are rocky objects that mostly orbit the Sun
original_text: Asteroids are rocky objects that mostly orbit the Sun.

text: comets are icy bodies that can grow glowing tails near the Sun
original_text: Comets are icy bodies that can grow glowing tails near the Sun.

text: meteoroids are small pieces of rock or metal traveling through space
original_text: Meteoroids are small pieces of rock or metal traveling through space.

text: A meteor is the streak of light produced when a space rock burns in Earth's atmosphere
original_text: A meteor is the streak of light produced when a space rock burns in Earth's atmosphere.

text: A meteorite is a space rock that reaches the ground
original_text: A meteorite is a space rock that reaches the ground.

text: gravity shapes the motion of planets, moons, stars, and galaxies
original_text: Gravity shapes the motion of planets, moons, stars, and galaxies.

text: orbits happen because gravity pulls objects inward while their motion carries them forward
original_text: Orbits happen because gravity pulls objects inward while their motion carries them forward.

text: the solar system contains the Sun and the objects bound to it by gravity
original_text: The solar system contains the Sun and the objects bound to it by gravity.

text: mercury is the closest planet to the Sun
original_text: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun.

text: venus has a thick atmosphere and extreme surface temperatures
original_text: Venus has a thick atmosphere and extreme surface temperatures.

text: earth is the only known planet with abundant surface liquid water and life
original_text: Earth is the only known planet with abundant surface liquid water and life.

text: mars is often called the red planet because of iron-rich dust
original_text: Mars is often called the red planet because of iron-rich dust.

text: jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system
original_text: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system.

text: saturn is famous for its large ring system
original_text: Saturn is famous for its large ring system.

text: uranus rotates on its side compared with most planets
original_text: Uranus rotates on its side compared with most planets.

text: neptune is a distant planet with powerful winds
original_text: Neptune is a distant planet with powerful winds.

text: dwarf planets are round objects that orbit the Sun but have not cleared their orbital neighborhoods
original_text: Dwarf planets are round objects that orbit the Sun but have not cleared their orbital neighborhoods.

text: pluto is classified as a dwarf planet
original_text: Pluto is classified as a dwarf planet.

text: A galaxy is a huge collection of stars, gas, dust, dark matter, and planetary systems
original_text: A galaxy is a huge collection of stars, gas, dust, dark matter, and planetary systems.

text: the Milky Way is the galaxy that contains our solar system
original_text: The Milky Way is the galaxy that contains our solar system.

text: galaxies can be spiral, elliptical, or irregular in shape
original_text: Galaxies can be spiral, elliptical, or irregular in shape.

text: the Andromeda Galaxy is the nearest large spiral galaxy to the Milky Way
original_text: The Andromeda Galaxy is the nearest large spiral galaxy to the Milky Way.

text: light-years measure distance, not time
original_text: Light-years measure distance, not time.

text: one light-year is the distance light travels in one year
original_text: One light-year is the distance light travels in one year.

text: because light takes time to travel, looking into space is also looking into the past
original_text: Because light takes time to travel, looking into space is also looking into the past.

text: telescopes collect light to reveal distant objects
original_text: Telescopes collect light to reveal distant objects.

text: optical telescopes observe visible light
original_text: Optical telescopes observe visible light.

text: radio telescopes observe radio waves from space
original_text: Radio telescopes observe radio waves from space.

text: space telescopes avoid many distortions caused by Earth's atmosphere
original_text: Space telescopes avoid many distortions caused by Earth's atmosphere.

text: spectroscopy lets astronomers study light to identify composition, motion, and temperature
original_text: Spectroscopy lets astronomers study light to identify composition, motion, and temperature.

text: redshift can show that an object is moving away
original_text: Redshift can show that an object is moving away.

text: blueshift can show that an object is moving closer
original_text: Blueshift can show that an object is moving closer.

text: the expanding universe means distant galaxies are generally moving away from us
original_text: The expanding universe means distant galaxies are generally moving away from us.

text: the Big Bang theory describes the early hot, dense state of the universe
original_text: The Big Bang theory describes the early hot, dense state of the universe.

text: the cosmic microwave background is leftover radiation from the early universe
original_text: The cosmic microwave background is leftover radiation from the early universe.

text: stars form inside clouds of gas and dust called nebulae
original_text: Stars form inside clouds of gas and dust called nebulae.

text: nebulae can be stellar nurseries, glowing gas clouds, or remnants of dead stars
original_text: Nebulae can be stellar nurseries, glowing gas clouds, or remnants of dead stars.

text: A protostar forms when gas and dust collapse under gravity
original_text: A protostar forms when gas and dust collapse under gravity.

text: main sequence stars fuse hydrogen into helium in their cores
original_text: Main sequence stars fuse hydrogen into helium in their cores.

text: A star's mass strongly affects its brightness, temperature, and lifetime
original_text: A star's mass strongly affects its brightness, temperature, and lifetime.

text: massive stars burn fuel quickly and have shorter lifetimes
original_text: Massive stars burn fuel quickly and have shorter lifetimes.

text: smaller stars burn fuel more slowly and can last much longer
original_text: Smaller stars burn fuel more slowly and can last much longer.

text: red giants are expanded stars near later stages of stellar life
original_text: Red giants are expanded stars near later stages of stellar life.

text: white dwarfs are dense remnants left by some stars after they shed outer layers
original_text: White dwarfs are dense remnants left by some stars after they shed outer layers.

text: supernovae are powerful stellar explosions
original_text: Supernovae are powerful stellar explosions.

text: supernovae can spread heavy elements into space
original_text: Supernovae can spread heavy elements into space.

text: neutron stars are extremely dense stellar remnants
original_text: Neutron stars are extremely dense stellar remnants.

text: pulsars are rapidly rotating neutron stars that emit beams of radiation
original_text: Pulsars are rapidly rotating neutron stars that emit beams of radiation.

text: black holes are regions where gravity is so strong that not even light can escape
original_text: Black holes are regions where gravity is so strong that not even light can escape.

text: the event horizon marks the boundary around a black hole beyond which escape is impossible
original_text: The event horizon marks the boundary around a black hole beyond which escape is impossible.

text: supermassive black holes are found at the centers of many galaxies
original_text: Supermassive black holes are found at the centers of many galaxies.

text: dark matter appears to add gravity without emitting light
original_text: Dark matter appears to add gravity without emitting light.

text: dark energy is linked to the accelerating expansion of the universe
original_text: Dark energy is linked to the accelerating expansion of the universe.

text: exoplanets are planets outside our solar system
original_text: Exoplanets are planets outside our solar system.

text: astronomers find some exoplanets by watching stars dim during transits
original_text: Astronomers find some exoplanets by watching stars dim during transits.

text: the habitable zone is the region where liquid water could exist on a planet's surface
original_text: The habitable zone is the region where liquid water could exist on a planet's surface.

text: A planet in the habitable zone is not automatically habitable
original_text: A planet in the habitable zone is not automatically habitable.

text: atmospheres strongly affect planetary temperatures and surface conditions
original_text: Atmospheres strongly affect planetary temperatures and surface conditions.

text: earth's atmosphere protects life from many harmful space hazards
original_text: Earth's atmosphere protects life from many harmful space hazards.

text: the Moon affects Earth's tides through gravity
original_text: The Moon affects Earth's tides through gravity.

text: lunar phases happen because we see different sunlit portions of the Moon
original_text: Lunar phases happen because we see different sunlit portions of the Moon.

text: solar eclipses occur when the Moon passes between Earth and the Sun
original_text: Solar eclipses occur when the Moon passes between Earth and the Sun.

text: lunar eclipses occur when Earth blocks sunlight from reaching the Moon
original_text: Lunar eclipses occur when Earth blocks sunlight from reaching the Moon.

text: seasons happen because Earth's axis is tilted as it orbits the Sun
original_text: Seasons happen because Earth's axis is tilted as it orbits the Sun.

text: day and night happen because Earth rotates
original_text: Day and night happen because Earth rotates.

text: A year is based on Earth's orbit around the Sun
original_text: A year is based on Earth's orbit around the Sun.

text: uses math, physics, observation, and modeling
original_text: Astronomy uses math, physics, observation, and modeling.

text: space probes let scientists study planets and moons directly
original_text: Space probes let scientists study planets and moons directly.

text: rovers explore planetary surfaces and send data back to Earth
original_text: Rovers explore planetary surfaces and send data back to Earth.

text: satellites help observe Earth and space
original_text: Satellites help observe Earth and space.

text: human spaceflight lets people live and work beyond Earth's surface
original_text: Human spaceflight lets people live and work beyond Earth's surface.

text: the International Space Station is a laboratory in low Earth orbit
original_text: The International Space Station is a laboratory in low Earth orbit.

text: rocket engines work by pushing mass backward to move forward
original_text: Rocket engines work by pushing mass backward to move forward.

text: helps explain where elements came from
original_text: Astronomy helps explain where elements came from.

text: many atoms in your body were formed inside stars or stellar explosions
original_text: Many atoms in your body were formed inside stars or stellar explosions.

text: connects tiny human life to enormous cosmic history
original_text: Astronomy connects tiny human life to enormous cosmic history.

text: the night sky is a record of distant light and ancient events
original_text: The night sky is a record of distant light and ancient events.

text: constellations are patterns humans assign to stars in the sky
original_text: Constellations are patterns humans assign to stars in the sky.

text: different cultures have created different stories from the same sky
original_text: Different cultures have created different stories from the same sky.

text: one of humanity's oldest sciences
original_text: Astronomy is one of humanity's oldest sciences.

text: modern astronomy studies objects far beyond what the eye can see
original_text: Modern astronomy studies objects far beyond what the eye can see.

text: astronomy often deals with scales too large for intuition
original_text: Astronomy often deals with scales too large for intuition.

text: the universe is much larger and stranger than everyday experience suggests
original_text: The universe is much larger and stranger than everyday experience suggests.

text: astronomers use evidence rather than guesses to understand distant objects
original_text: Astronomers use evidence rather than guesses to understand distant objects.

text: space is mostly empty, but not completely empty
original_text: Space is mostly empty, but not completely empty.

text: gas and dust between stars can form future stars and planets
original_text: Gas and dust between stars can form future stars and planets.

text: planetary systems can look very different from our solar system
original_text: Planetary systems can look very different from our solar system.

text: astronomy keeps finding objects that challenge earlier assumptions

text: astronomy the science of staring into the giant dark sauce
original_text: The meatball considers astronomy the science of staring into the giant dark sauce.

text: turns points of light into stories about matter, time, and gravity
original_text: Astronomy turns points of light into stories about matter, time, and gravity.

text: makes earth feel small without making it unimportant
original_text: Astronomy makes Earth feel small without making it unimportant.

text: cosmic detective work with very old light
